name: DTT (Dithiothreitol, DL-Dithiothreitol)
striking_fact: DTT in solution oxidizes and loses reducing power within ~1 week at 4°C — make fresh on the day of use for sensitive applications.

sds_facts.storage: Store powder at −20°C, desiccated. Aqueous stocks may be stored at −20°C in single-use aliquots; discard after thawing once.

bench_knowledge: DTT oxidizes rapidly in aqueous solution exposed to air — the disulfide form is inactive. For critical reductions (e.g., elution of disulfide-linked proteins), always make a fresh stock on the day of use.
At 4°C in degassed or nitrogen-purged buffer, DTT stock solutions retain activity for ~1 week; at room temperature in air-exposed buffer, activity drops within hours.
Typical working concentrations: 0.5–5 mM in enzymatic reactions; 10–100 mM for protein denaturation/reduction before gel electrophoresis.

DTT is incompatible with maleimide-based dyes and crosslinkers — it will quench the maleimide before it can react with your cysteine. Add DTT only when you want to reduce, never when you want to label.
TCEP is a non-thiol alternative that is stable in solution at room temperature for months and compatible at low pH. Use TCEP when stability matters more than cost.
Aliquot powder stocks into small tubes, backfill with nitrogen if available, and seal with Parafilm before returning to −20°C to limit oxidation on each opening.
